skill.name: dice-easy-apply
skill.description: Automate Dice.com Easy Apply searches and applications with Puppeteer/Chromium, resume and cover-letter uploads, remote-role filtering, and conservative application guardrails.
skill.summary: Automate Dice.com Easy Apply job searches and applications using Puppeteer browser automation with configurable search filters and application safeguards.
skill.author: ralyodio

security_audit.summary: This skill automates Dice.com job applications using Puppeteer browser automation. Static analysis flagged 59 potential issues, many of which are false positives from the documentation format. Actual security concerns include environment variable access for file paths, temporary file usage, and platform ToS compliance. The skill is not malicious but presents elevated risk due to automated job application behavior that may trigger account penalties on Dice.com.

high_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Sensitive File Paths
high_findings.description: The skill references environment variables (RESUME_PDF, COVER_PDF, CHROME_PROFILE) that point to sensitive user files. While the skill explicitly warns against storing credentials in scripts, the design requires these environment variables to be set with paths to private documents.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variables pointing to user files (resume, cover letter) are legitimate configuration patterns, but improper handling could expose sensitive documents.

medium_findings.title: Temporary Directory File Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill writes state and log files to /tmp directories which have predictable paths and are accessible to other processes on multi-tenant systems.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Using /tmp for state files is a common pattern but creates potential data leakage risk on shared systems.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution via Test Commands
medium_findings.description: Bash test commands are used to verify file existence before processing. While hardcoded, this pattern enables shell command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The test commands are for file validation and not directly user-controllable, but the pattern enables command execution.

medium_findings.title: Browser Automation with Chromium
medium_findings.description: Puppeteer launches a visible Chromium browser instance with sandbox disabled for container compatibility. This reduces browser isolation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The --disable-setuid-sandbox flag is common for containerized environments but reduces security boundaries.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - Documentation Format
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' at multiple lines in SKILL.md, but this is a Markdown documentation file containing example code snippets, not executable Ruby code. The backtick pattern detection was triggered by code fence formatting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is a documentation file; no backtick command execution exists. Static scanner misidentified code fences as execution patterns.

low_findings.title: Network Access - Job Board URLs
low_findings.description: The skill accesses Dice.com URLs for job searches and applications. This is expected behavior for a job automation tool, not credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Accessing job board URLs is the intended functionality of this skill, not network-based credential theft.

low_findings.title: Hidden File Access - Chrome Profile Directory
low_findings.description: The skill accesses ~/.cache/dice-chrome for persistent browser login state. This is a standard pattern for browser automation requiring manual authentication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Chrome profile storage in ~/.cache is standard behavior; the skill uses this for credential-free authentication.

content.user_title: Automate Dice.com Easy Apply Job Applications
content.value_statement: Job seekers spend hours manually applying to positions on Dice.com. This skill automates search, filtering, and application submission using Puppeteer browser automation, helping users efficiently apply to remote roles with verified resumes.
actual_capabilities: Search Dice.com using configurable remote and Easy Apply filters
Extract job IDs and descriptions from search results
Apply to jobs using Dice Easy Apply wizard with verified resume upload
Skip jobs with onsite, hybrid, or clearance requirements automatically
Maintain application state to avoid duplicate submissions
Handle CAPTCHA and MFA prompts by pausing for human intervention
limitations: Cannot bypass Dice.com anti-bot detection or CAPTCHAs
May trigger account penalties if applied too aggressively
Requires manual Chrome login for initial authentication
Cannot handle complex application forms requiring dynamic content

use_cases.title: Passive Job Application Monitoring
use_cases.description: Set up daily runs to search new remote AI and software engineering roles on Dice.com and apply automatically within defined limits.
use_cases.target_user: Active job seekers looking for remote positions

use_cases.title: Bulk Resume Distribution
use_cases.description: Configure search queries for multiple roles and let the skill handle the repetitive application process while ensuring each application uses the verified resume.
use_cases.target_user: Job seekers wanting to apply to multiple similar positions quickly

use_cases.title: Remote-Focused Job Filtering
use_cases.description: Use the skill to find and apply only to fully remote positions, filtering out hybrid and onsite roles that do not match work preferences.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Dice Job Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the dice-easy-apply skill to search for remote AI engineer positions on Dice.com. Set MAX_APPLY to 5 and DRY_RUN to 1 for review.

prompt_templates.title: Full Stack Developer Applications
prompt_templates.prompt: Run dice-easy-apply with queries for 'full stack engineer', 'react developer', and 'typescript engineer'. Apply to up to 10 positions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - applying to multiple role types

prompt_templates.title: Contract Position Filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for contract remote positions using EMPLOYMENT_FILTER=CONTRACTS. Focus on software engineering roles matching the resume wheelhouse.

prompt_templates.title: Resume-Specific Job Matching
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the configured RESUME_PDF and COVER_PDF paths. Search for roles matching the resume skills. Apply only to positions within the resume wheelhouse.

output_examples.input: Search for gen ai engineer remote positions, apply to 3 jobs
output_examples.output:
Submitted: Senior GenAI Engineer — TechCorp — https://www.dice.com/jobs/detail/12345
Skipped: ML Engineer — DataInc — https://www.dice.com/jobs/detail/12346 — not_remote_only_or_has_hybrid_location_text

State: /tmp/dice-easyapply-daily/state.json

output_examples.input: Run dry run for full stack react positions
output_examples.output:
DRY RUN - Would submit to:
- Full Stack React Engineer — WebDev Co — https://www.dice.com/jobs/detail/12347
- Senior React Developer — StartupXYZ — https://www.dice.com/jobs/detail/12348

Review these applications before setting DRY_RUN=0

best_practices: Start with DRY_RUN=1 to review applications before submission
Keep MAX_APPLY at 15 or fewer to avoid account penalties
Use a separate Chrome profile for automated applications to isolate from manual browsing
anti_patterns: Do not set MAX_APPLY above 20 — high application volume may trigger Dice.com account restrictions
Do not skip the CAPTCHA handling steps — this protects your account from being locked
Do not reuse credentials in multiple scripts — always use environment variables or pre-authenticated profiles

faq.question: Will Dice.com ban my account for using this automation?
faq.answer: Dice.com may restrict accounts that submit too many applications in a short period. Keep MAX_APPLY below 15 and use conservative application pacing.

faq.question: How do I handle CAPTCHA challenges?
faq.answer: The skill pauses when it detects CAPTCHA or MFA prompts and logs them for manual resolution before continuing.

faq.question: Can I use this for other job boards?
faq.answer: This skill is specifically designed for Dice.com Easy Apply. Other job boards would require separate skill implementations.

faq.question: How do I configure my resume and cover letter?
faq.answer: Set the RESUME_PDF and COVER_PDF environment variables to absolute paths of your PDF documents.

faq.question: What happens if the browser session closes during application?
faq.answer: The skill detects closed sessions and creates a fresh page, re-registering handlers before continuing from the saved state.

faq.question: How do I review applications before they are submitted?
faq.answer: Set DRY_RUN=1 to run in review mode. The skill will show what it would submit without making actual applications.
